title: SSH key login rejected: authorized_keys permissions too open
description_md:
Key-based SSH login for the `deploy` user suddenly stopped working — it falls through to asking for a password (which deploy doesn't have). The key and `authorized_keys` are definitely in place. A backup-restore reset a bunch of file modes last night. SSH in as `trainee` (passwordless `sudo`), find why sshd is refusing the key, and fix it.

A private key for testing is at `~/deploy_key` — try `ssh -i ~/deploy_key deploy@localhost`. You're done when that key login succeeds.
